rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim is materially weakened by a factual contradiction: the artifact identifies GPT-3 as the 'current frontier model' and 'state of the art' while placing the pilot study in 'late 2024,' when GPT-3 is far from frontier. Strengthening requires reanchoring the thesis to a defensible scope claim.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain from 'AI-tell prior' to 'calibration recovers accuracy' to 'protocol generalizes to systems readers deploy' has a load-bearing gap: calibration is shown to help on GPT-3 specifically, but the generalization claim to other frontier models is asserted rather than argued. 'Make stronger' plausibly targets this gap.
rationale.R1.6: 'Make this stronger' is a common prose-improvement directive; sentence-level tightening, hedge reduction, and more assertive framing of the 51.4%→58.1% finding are all natural targets.

parsed.clarification_question: By 'stronger,' do you primarily mean (A) improving prose quality — word choice, sentence rhythm, assertiveness of framing — or (B) tightening the logical argumentation and fixing the internal consistency issues (e.g., GPT-3 described as frontier in a 2024 study)?
